prompt: We need to document Git's config file format (gitconfig / INI dialect) so we can write a Go parser for it. The reference is https://git-scm.com/docs/git-config under the FILES / CONFIGURATION FILE / SYNTAX section. Extract a SPEC.md into ./gitconfig-spec/ covering: section headers (with optional subsections in double quotes), variable names and their case rules, value types (boolean / integer / color / path-expandable / string with escapes), comments (# and ;), line continuation with backslash, and the include / includeIf mechanism. Be careful — gitconfig has several places where the spec is loose or where multiple shells/clients disagree; use Ambiguity callouts where appropriate.

assertions.text: SPEC.md exists at the root of the output directory
no leftover scratch files (_spec.txt, _spec.html, _toc.md, _spec_part_*.md, _sections_index.md) in the output directory
the six top-level sections appear in this exact order: Overview, Lexical Elements (Tokens), Structure (Grammar), Semantics, Examples, Appendix
section headers with quoted subsections like [http "https://example.com/"] are documented
both # and ; are documented as comment introducers
line continuation via trailing backslash is documented
value types boolean, integer (with k/M/G suffix), and at least one of color/path are documented
case-insensitivity of section/variable names is documented (and case-sensitivity of subsection names if the doc states it)
include / includeIf directive is documented under Semantics or Grammar
at least one `> **Ambiguity:**` callout is present, since the gitconfig doc is genuinely loose in places
the Examples section contains at least three distinct gitconfig documents
